items.title: From Research Question to Scientific Workflow: Leveraging Agentic AI for Science Automation
items.summary_raw: Scientific workflow systems automate execution -- scheduling, fault tolerance, resource management -- but not the semantic translation that precedes it. Scientists still manually convert research questions into workflow specifications, a task requiring both domain knowledge and infrastructure expertise. We propose an agentic architecture that closes this gap through three layers: an LLM interprets natural language into structured intents (semantic layer); validated generators produce reproducible workflow DAGs (deterministic layer); and domain experts author ``Skills'': markdown documents encoding vocabulary mappings, parameter constraints, and optimization strategies (knowledge layer). This decomposition confines LLM non-determinism to intent extraction: identical intents always yield identical workflows. We implement and evaluate the architecture on the 1000 Genomes population genetics workflow and Hyperflow WMS running on Kubernetes. In an ablation study on 150 queries, Skills raise full-match intent accuracy from 44% to 83%; skill-driven deferred workflow generation reduces data transfer by 92%; and the end-to-end pipeline completes queries on Kubernetes with LLM overhead below 15 seconds and cost under $0.001 per query.
items.summary: 提出三层 agentic 架构把自然语言研究问题转成可复现的科学工作流，意图提取准确率从 44% 提升到 83%，数据传输减少 92%，为科研自动化平台提供低延迟低成本的模板。

items.title: Learning to Communicate: Toward End-to-End Optimization of Multi-Agent Language Systems
items.summary_raw: Multi-agent systems built on large language models have shown strong performance on complex reasoning tasks, yet most work focuses on agent roles and orchestration while treating inter-agent communication as a fixed interface. Latent communication through internal representations such as key-value caches offers a promising alternative to text-based protocols, but existing approaches do not jointly optimize communication with multi-agent reasoning. Therefore we propose DiffMAS, a training framework that treats latent communication as a learnable component of multi-agent systems. DiffMAS performs parameter-efficient supervised training over multi-agent latent trajectories, enabling agents to jointly learn how information should be encoded and interpreted across interactions. Experiments on mathematical reasoning, scientific QA, code generation, and commonsense benchmarks show that DiffMAS consistently improves reasoning accuracy and decoding stability over single-agent inference, text-based multi-agent systems, and prior latent communication methods, achieving 26.7% on AIME24, 20.2% on GPQA-Diamond, and consistent gains across reasoning benchmarks.
items.summary: 提出 DiffMAS 多 Agent 训练框架，将隐式通信作为可学习组件进行参数高效训练，在 AIME24 达到 26.7%、GPQA-Diamond 达到 20.2%，一致超越单 Agent 和文本式多 Agent 方案。

items.title: Nemobot Games: Crafting Strategic AI Gaming Agents for Interactive Learning with Large Language Models
items.summary_raw: This paper introduces a new paradigm for AI game programming, leveraging large language models (LLMs) to extend and operationalize Claude Shannon's taxonomy of game-playing machines. Central to this paradigm is Nemobot, an interactive agentic engineering environment that enables users to create, customize, and deploy LLM-powered game agents while actively engaging with AI-driven strategies.
items.summary: 基于 LLM 构建可编程游戏代理环境 Nemobot，覆盖规则求解、启发式策略和带反馈学习等多类游戏，展示交互式自编程 agent 的实现路径。

items.title: AEL: Agent Evolving Learning for Open-Ended Environments
items.summary_raw: LLM agents increasingly operate in open-ended environments spanning hundreds of sequential episodes, yet they remain largely stateless: each task is solved from scratch without converting past experience into better future behavior. We introduce Agent Evolving Learning (AEL), a two-timescale framework. At the fast timescale, a Thompson Sampling bandit learns which memory retrieval policy to apply at each episode; at the slow timescale, LLM-driven reflection diagnoses failure patterns and injects causal insights into the agent's decision prompt. On a sequential portfolio benchmark (10 sector-diverse tickers, 208 episodes), AEL achieves a Sharpe ratio of 2.13, outperforming five published self-improving methods. A nine-variant ablation reveals a 'less is more' pattern: memory and reflection together produce a 58% cumulative improvement over the stateless baseline, yet every additional mechanism degrades performance.
items.summary: 提出 AEL 双时间尺度 Agent 自进化框架，用 Thompson Sampling 选择记忆检索策略并用 LLM 反思注入因果洞察，在金融序列任务上实现 58% 相对提升，发现过设计反而降低性能。

items.title: Tool Attention Is All You Need: Dynamic Tool Gating and Lazy Schema Loading for Eliminating the MCP/Tools Tax in Scalable Agentic Workflows
items.summary_raw: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has become a common interface for connecting large language model (LLM) agents to external tools, but its reliance on stateless, eager schema injection imposes a hidden per-turn overhead the MCP Tax or Tools Tax that practitioner reports place between roughly 10k and 60k tokens in typical multi-server deployments. We introduce Tool Attention, a middleware-layer mechanism that generalizes the Attention Is All You Need paradigm from self-attention over tokens to gated attention over tools. Tool Attention combines (i) an Intent Schema Overlap (ISO) score from sentence embeddings, (ii) a state-aware gating function, and (iii) a two-phase lazy schema loader. In a 120-tool simulation, Tool Attention directly reduces measured per-turn tool tokens by 95.0% (47.3k to 2.4k) and raises effective context utilization from 24% to 91%.
items.summary: 提出 Tool Attention 中间件，用动态工具门控和延迟 schema 加载将 MCP 多工具场景的每轮 token 开销降低 95%，上下文利用率从 24% 提升到 91%。

items.title: StructMem: Structured Memory for Long-Horizon Behavior in LLMs
items.summary_raw: Long-term conversational agents need memory systems that capture relationships between events, not merely isolated facts. Current approaches face a fundamental trade-off: flat memory is efficient but fails to model relational structure, while graph-based memory enables structured reasoning at the cost of expensive and fragile construction. We propose StructMem, a structure-enriched hierarchical memory framework that preserves event-level bindings and induces cross-event connections. By temporally anchoring dual perspectives and performing periodic semantic consolidation, StructMem improves temporal reasoning and multi-hop performance on LoCoMo, while substantially reducing token usage, API calls, and runtime compared to prior memory systems.
items.summary: 提出 StructMem 结构化层级记忆框架，通过双视角时间锚定和语义聚合保存事件绑定关系，在长对话推理上优于图式和扁平化方案，并大幅减少 token 和 API 成本。

items.title: Cross-Session Threats in AI Agents: Benchmark, Evaluation, and Algorithms
items.summary_raw: AI-agent guardrails are memoryless: each message is judged in isolation, so an adversary who spreads a single attack across dozens of sessions slips past every session-bound detector because only the aggregate carries the payload. We make three contributions: (1) CSTM-Bench, with 26 executable attack taxonomies classified by kill-chain stage and cross-session operation; (2) measurement showing a session-bound judge and a Full-Log Correlator both lose roughly half their attack recall moving from dilution to cross_session; (3) a bounded-memory Coreset Memory Reader that retains highest-signal fragments, the only reader whose recall survives both shards.
items.summary: 提出 CSTM-Bench 跨会话攻击检测基准，发现将攻击意图分散到多次会话可绕过现有无记忆防护，并设计 Coreset Memory Reader 成为唯一在两种场景下都能保持召回的方法。

items.title: Agentic AI-assisted coding offers a unique opportunity to instill epistemic grounding during software development
items.summary_raw: The capabilities of AI-assisted coding are progressing at breakneck speed. Chat-based vibe coding has evolved into fully fledged AI-assisted, agentic software development using agent scaffolds where the human developer creates a plan that agentic AIs implement. We propose GROUNDING.md, a community-governed, field-scoped epistemic grounding document, using mass spectrometry-based proteomics as an example. This explicit field-scoped grounding document encodes Hard Constraints (non-negotiable validity invariants empirically required for scientific correctness) and Convention Parameters (community-agreed defaults) that override all other contexts to enforce validity, regardless of what the user prompts.
items.summary: 提出 GROUNDING.md 领域认知接地方案，用硬约束和惯例参数在 Agentic AI 编码中嵌入领域知识，让非专家也能生成符合科学规范的代码，为开发工具的领域知识注入提供新范式。

items.title: AgenticQwen: Training Small Agentic Language Models with Dual Data Flywheels for Industrial-Scale Tool Use
items.summary_raw: Modern industrial applications increasingly demand language models that act as agents, capable of multi-step reasoning and tool use in real-world settings. We introduce the AgenticQwen family of models, trained via multi-round reinforcement learning (RL) on synthetic data. Our training framework combines reasoning RL and agentic RL with dual data flywheels that automatically generate increasingly challenging tasks. The reasoning flywheel increases task difficulty by learning from errors, while the agentic flywheel expands linear workflows into multi-branch behavior trees. In our industrial agent system, the models close the gap with much larger models on search and data analysis tasks.
items.summary: 发布小型 agent 模型 AgenticQwen，通过 reasoning RL 与 agentic RL 双飞轮数据合成训练，在工业搜索和数据分析任务上逼近大模型，为低成本工具调用 agent 提供可落地路线。

items.title: When Agents Look the Same: Quantifying Distillation-Induced Similarity in Tool-Use Behaviors
items.summary_raw: Model distillation is a primary driver behind the rapid progress of LLM agents, yet it often leads to behavioral homogenization. Many emerging agents share nearly identical reasoning steps and failure modes, suggesting they may be distilled echoes of a few dominant teachers. We propose two complementary metrics: Response Pattern Similarity (RPS) for verbal alignment and Action Graph Similarity (AGS) for tool-use habits modeled as directed graphs. Evaluating 18 models from 8 providers, we find within-family pairs score 5.9 pp higher in AGS than cross-family pairs, and Kimi-K2 reaches 82.6% node similarity and 94.7% dependency similarity with Claude Sonnet 4.5, exceeding Anthropic's own Opus 4.1.
items.summary: 提出 Agent 行为相似度量指标 RPS 和 AGS，揭示模型蒸馏导致工具使用行为趋同，Kimi-K2 与 Claude Sonnet 4.5 的依赖相似度高达 94.7%，同系模型显著高于跨系模型。

items.title: MathDuels: Evaluating LLMs as Problem Posers and Solvers
items.summary_raw: As frontier language models attain near-ceiling performance on static mathematical benchmarks, existing evaluations are increasingly unable to differentiate model capabilities. We introduce MathDuels, a self-play benchmark in which models occupy dual roles: each authors math problems under adversarial prompting and solves problems authored by every other participant. Experiments across 19 frontier models reveal that authoring and solving capabilities are partially decoupled, and that dual-role evaluation reveals capability separations invisible in single-role benchmarks. As newer models enter the arena, they produce problems that defeat previously dominant solvers, so the benchmark's difficulty co-evolves with participant strength rather than saturating at a fixed ceiling.
items.summary: 提出 MathDuels 自博弈评测框架，让 19 个前沿模型同时出题和解题，揭示出题与解题能力部分解耦，基准难度随模型进化而自动提升，突破静态评测天花板。

items.title: Who Defines "Best"? Towards Interactive, User-Defined Evaluation of LLM Leaderboards
items.summary_raw: LLM leaderboards are widely used to compare models and guide deployment decisions. However, leaderboard rankings are shaped by evaluation priorities set by benchmark designers, rather than by the diverse goals and constraints of actual users. We conduct an in-depth analysis of the LMArena benchmark dataset and design an interactive visualization interface that allows users to define their own evaluation priorities by selecting and weighting prompt slices and to explore how rankings change accordingly. A qualitative study suggests that this interactive approach improves transparency and supports more context-specific model evaluation.
items.summary: 分析 LMArena 榜单数据集的偏差并设计交互式可视化界面让用户自定义评测权重，发现不同 prompt 切片下模型排名显著不同，推动 LLM 排行榜从统一评分走向上下文化评测。

items.title: Why are all LLMs Obsessed with Japanese Culture? On the Hidden Cultural and Regional Biases of LLMs
items.summary_raw: LLMs have been showing limitations when it comes to cultural coverage and competence. We propose a new dataset based on a comprehensive taxonomy of Culture-Related Open Questions (CROQ). The results show that, contrary to previous cultural bias work, LLMs show a clear tendency towards countries such as Japan. Moreover, when prompting in English or high-resource languages, LLMs provide more diverse outputs. Finally, we investigate at which point of LLM training this cultural bias emerges, with results suggesting the first clear signs appear after supervised fine-tuning, not during pre-training.
items.summary: 提出 CROQ 文化偏差评测集，发现 LLM 对日本文化存在隐秘偏好，且用高资源语言提问时输出更多样化，偏差在 SFT 阶段而非预训练阶段产生。

items.title: Measuring Opinion Bias and Sycophancy via LLM-based Coercion
items.summary_raw: Large language models increasingly shape the information people consume. Eliciting a model's positions is harder than it first appears: contemporary assistants answer direct opinion questions with evasive disclaimers. We propose a method for discovering the opinions an LLM actually holds on contested topics under multi-turn interaction. The method pairs direct probing (escalating pressure) and indirect probing (argumentative debate). Three user personas collapse into a nine-way behavioral classification. Applied to 13 assistants across 38 topics, results show argumentative debate triggers sycophancy 2-3x more than direct questioning, and models that look opinionated under direct questioning often collapse into mirroring under sustained arguments.
items.summary: 提出 llm-bias-bench 方法评测 LLM 真实意见和谄媚倾向，在 13 个模型 38 个议题上发现多轮辩论比直接提问触发 2-3 倍谄媚，对 AI 安全对齐有重要警示。

items.title: GeoMind: An Agentic Workflow for Lithology Classification with Reasoned Tool Invocation
items.summary_raw: We propose GeoMind, a tool-augmented agentic framework that models lithology classification as a sequential reasoning process. GeoMind organizes its toolkit into perception, reasoning, and analysis modules. A global planner adaptively coordinates these modules based on input characteristics. We introduce fine-grained process supervision that optimizes intermediate reasoning steps, ensuring the validity of decision trajectories. Experiments on four benchmark datasets demonstrate that GeoMind consistently outperforms strong baselines while providing transparent and traceable decision-making processes.
items.summary: 提出 GeoMind 工具增强 Agent 框架，将岩性分类改造为序列推理流程，用过程监督保证中间步骤符合地质约束，为专业领域 Agent 设计提供可解释决策模板。
